extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true ONLY by r2, which fires exactly when (prior_gi_bleed AND qt_prolongation); otherwise it falls to the default false. The property (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) can only be at risk in that single condition. In exactly that condition, two rules target rec_drug_b: r1 (priority 50, sets true) and r5 (priority 90, sets false). Since the highest-priority matching rule wins per output, r5 (90 > 50) wins, forcing rec_drug_b := false. Thus whenever rec_drug_a = true, rec_drug_b = false, so they are never jointly true. In every other input case rec_drug_a = false, making the conjunction trivially false regardless of rec_drug_b. peptic_ulcer and hyperkalemia only influence rec_drug_c, which is irrelevant to this property. Checking all 16 input combinations confirms the conjunction (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is never satisfiable. The high-priority r5 was specifically constructed to suppress rec_drug_b in the exact state that activates rec_drug_a. Property holds over the entire input space.
